action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|...#.A...|
 2|...B.#...|
 3|#..###...|
 4|.##..#.p#|
 5|........2|
 6|.....x...|
 7|.S.y#.#..|
 8|q......1#|
 9|.##...#.#|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (2,7) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (2,7), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (6,1): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {0}. So it is open at t=0,2,... and closed at t=1,3,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (4,2): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {1, 2}. So it is open at t=1,2,5,6,... and closed at t=0,3,4,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (8,4); t mod 8=1: (8,5); t mod 8=2: (8,6); t mod 8=3: (8,7); t mod 8=4: (9,7); t mod 8=5: (8,7); t mod 8=6: (8,6); t mod 8=7: (8,5). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (1,8); t mod 4=1: (2,8); t mod 4=2: (3,8); t mod 4=3: (2,8). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (8,8) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (6,6) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (9,5) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (4,7) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 35 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 7 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (2,7) at t=0. I need to pick up Parcel 1 at (8,8) and deliver it to (6,6). I also need to pick up Parcel 2 at (9,5) and deliver it to (4,7).
First, let's check the guards' positions at t=0 and t=1 to ensure I don't get caught immediately.
Guard P at t=0 (0 mod 8 = 0): (8,4).
Guard Q at t=0 (0 mod 4 = 0): (1,8).
My path to Parcel 1 (8,8) involves moving East along row 7 or 8.
Row 7: (2,7) -> ... -> (7,7) is open, but (8,7) is a wall '#'. So I cannot go directly East along row 7 to column 8.
Row 8: (2,8) is '.', (3,8) is '.', (4,8) is '.', (5,8) is '.', (6,8) is '.', (7,8) is '.', (8,8) is '1'. This path is clear of walls.
However, Guard Q is at (1,8) at t=0 and moves to (2,8) at t=1, (3,8) at t=2, (2,8) at t=3, then repeats.
If I move East along row 8 starting at t=0:
t=0: Move E to (3,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
t=1: Move E to (4,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
t=2: Move E to (5,8). Guard Q moves to (3,8). Safe.
t=3: Move E to (6,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
t=4: Move E to (7,8). Guard Q moves to (3,8). Safe.
t=5: Move E to (8,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
At t=5, I arrive at (8,8) and pick up Parcel 1.
Now I need to go to (6,6) to deliver Parcel 1.
From (8,8), I can go West to (6,8), then North to (6,7) (which is '#', wait, let's check the map).
Map Row 6: `.....x...` -> (6,6) is 'x'. (6,7) is '#'.
Map Row 7: `.S.y#.#..` -> (6,7) is '#'.
So I cannot go North from (6,8) to (6,7).
Let's re-examine the map around (6,6).
(6,6) is 'x'.
Neighbors of (6,6):
N: (5,6) '.'
S: (7,6) '.'
E: (7,6) is '#', wait. Row 6 is `.....x...`. Columns: 123456789.
(1,6). (2,6). (3,6). (4,6). (5,6). (6,6)=x. (7,6). (8,6). (9,6).
Row 6 is all dots except x. So (7,6) and (8,6) are '.'.
Row 7: (1,7). (2,7)=S. (3,7). (4,7)=y. (5,7)=#. (6,7). (7,7). (8,7). (9,7).
Wait, the map string is `.S.y#.#..`.
Col 1: .
Col 2: S
Col 3: .
Col 4: y
Col 5: #
Col 6: .
Col 7: #
Col 8: .
Col 9: .
So (6,7) is '.', (7,7) is '#', (8,7) is '.'.
Okay, so from (6,8) I can go South to (6,7) which is '.', then South to (6,6) which is 'x'.
Let's re-verify the path from (8,8) to (6,6).
(8,8) -> W to (7,8) -> W to (6,8) -> S to (6,7) -> S to (6,6).
This path seems clear of walls.
Let's check Guard P's path again.
P cycle: 0:(8,4), 1:(8,5), 2:(8,6), 3:(8,7), 4:(9,7), 5:(8,7), 6:(8,6), 7:(8,5).
If I am at (8,8) at t=5.
t=6: Move W to (7,8). Guard P is at (8,6). Safe.
t=7: Move W to (6,8). Guard P is at (8,7). Safe.
t=8: Move S to (6,7). Guard P is at (8,6). Safe.
t=9: Move S to (6,6). Guard P is at (8,5). Safe.
At t=9, I deliver Parcel 1.
Now I need to go to (9,5) for Parcel 2.
From (6,6):
(6,6) -> E to (7,6) -> E to (8,6) -> S to (8,7) -> S to (8,8) -> E to (9,8) -> N to (9,7) -> N to (9,6) -> N to (9,5).
Wait, (9,5) is '2'.
Let's check walls on this path.
Row 6: all dots. (7,6), (8,6) are dots.
Row 7: (8,7) is dot. (9,7) is dot.
Row 8: (9,8) is dot.
Row 9: ` .##...#.# ` -> (1,9). (2,9)#. (3,9)#. (4,9). (5,9). (6,9). (7,9)#. (8,9). (9,9).
So (9,8) is above (9,9). (9,9) is dot.
(9,7) is above (9,8).
(9,6) is above (9,7).
(9,5) is above (9,6).
Is there a wall at (9,6)? Row 6 col 9 is dot.
Is there a wall at (9,7)? Row 7 col 9 is dot.
Is there a wall at (9,8)? Row 8 col 9 is dot.
Is there a wall at (9,9)? Row 9 col 9 is dot.
So the path (6,6)->(7,6)->(8,6)->(8,7)->(8,8)->(9,8)->(9,7)->(9,6)->(9,5) seems valid.
However, Guard P is at (8,6) at t=2, (8,7) at t=3, (8,6) at t=4, (8,7) at t=5, (8,6) at t=6, (8,7) at t=7, (8,6) at t=8, (8,7) at t=9.
If I arrive at (8,6) at t=10, Guard P is at (8,6) at t=10 (10 mod 8 = 2). That would be a bump/caught.
So I must avoid (8,6) at t=10.
Alternative path from (6,6) to (9,5)?
Maybe go via (7,6) -> (7,7) is wall '#'. So cannot go right from (7,6).
Must go (6,6) -> (7,6) -> (8,6) is the bottleneck.
Can I go (6,6) -> (5,6) -> (5,7) is '#'. No.
(6,6) -> (6,7) -> (7,7) is '#'. No.
(6,6) -> (6,7) -> (6,8) -> (7,8) -> (8,8) -> (9,8) -> (9,7) -> (9,6) -> (9,5).
Let's trace this second path.
t=10: At (6,6). Move E to (7,6). Guard P at (8,6). Safe.
t=11: At (7,6). Move E to (8,6). Guard P at (8,7) (11 mod 8 = 3). Safe.
t=12: At (8,6). Move S to (8,7). Guard P at (8,6) (12 mod 8 = 4). Safe.
t=13: At (8,7). Move S to (8,8). Guard P at (8,7) (13 mod 8 = 5). Safe.
t=14: At (8,8). Move E to (9,8). Guard P at (8,6) (14 mod 8 = 6). Safe.
t=15: At (9,8). Move N to (9,7). Guard P at (8,7) (15 mod 8 = 7). Safe.
t=16: At (9,7). Move N to (9,6). Guard P at (8,6) (16 mod 8 = 0). Safe.
t=17: At (9,6). Move N to (9,5). Guard P at (8,5) (17 mod 8 = 1). Safe.
At t=17, I pick up Parcel 2.
Now deliver to (4,7).
Path from (9,5) to (4,7).
(9,5) -> W to (8,5) -> W to (7,5) -> W to (6,5) -> W to (5,5) -> W to (4,5) -> S to (4,6) -> S to (4,7).
Check walls:
Row 5: `........2` -> all dots. (4,5) is dot.
Row 4: `.##..#.p#` -> (1,4). (2,4)#. (3,4)#. (4,4). (5,4). (6,4)#. (7,4). (8,4)p. (9,4)#.
So (4,5) is above (4,4) which is dot.
(4,4) is above (4,3) which is '#'.
Wait, I need to go to (4,7).
(4,7) is 'y'.
Row 7: (4,7) is dot.
Row 6: (4,6) is dot.
Row 5: (4,5) is dot.
So vertical column 4 from row 5 to 7 is clear.
Horizontal row 5 from 9 to 4 is clear.
Path: (9,5) -> (8,5) -> (7,5) -> (6,5) -> (5,5) -> (4,5) -> (4,6) -> (4,7).
Check Guard P positions:
t=17: At (9,5).
t=18: (8,5). Guard P at (8,5) (18 mod 8 = 2). **BUMP/CAUGHT**.
Guard P is at (8,5) at t=2, 10, 18...
So I cannot be at (8,5) at t=18.
I need to adjust the timing or path.
Maybe wait at (9,5) for one tick?
t=17: Arrive (9,5). Pick up.
t=18: WAIT. Guard P moves to (8,6). I stay at (9,5). Safe.
t=19: Move W to (8,5). Guard P at (8,7). Safe.
t=20: Move W to (7,5). Guard P at (8,6). Safe.
t=21: Move W to (6,5). Guard P at (8,5). Safe.
t=22: Move W to (5,5). Guard P at (8,7). Safe.
t=23: Move W to (4,5). Guard P at (8,6). Safe.
t=24: Move S to (4,6). Guard P at (8,5). Safe.
t=25: Move S to (4,7). Guard P at (8,7). Safe.
At t=25, deliver Parcel 2.
Total time: 25 ticks. Well within 35.
Now I need to plan the moves in chunks of 5.
Current position: (2,7) at t=0.
Plan 1: Go to (8,8).
Moves: E, E, E, E, E.
t=0: (2,7) -> E -> (3,8).
t=1: (3,8) -> E -> (4,8).
t=2: (4,8) -> E -> (5,8).
t=3: (5,8) -> E -> (6,8).
t=4: (6,8) -> E -> (7,8).
Wait, I calculated earlier that arriving at (8,8) takes 5 moves (t=0 to t=5).
So Plan 1 should be 5 moves to get to (7,8) at t=4? No, the prompt says "After each plan you receive a per-tick log...". The plan is 5 moves.
If I do E E E E E starting at t=0:
t=0: Move E to (3,8).
t=1: Move E to (4,8).
t=2: Move E to (5,8).
t=3: Move E to (6,8).
t=4: Move E to (7,8).
End of Plan 1: Position (7,8) at t=5.
Then Plan 2: E, S, S. (3 moves).
t=5: E to (8,8). Pick up P1.
t=6: S to (8,7).
t=7: S to (8,6).
End of Plan 2: Position (8,6) at t=8.
Then Plan 3: W, W, W, W, W. (5 moves).
t=8: W to (7,6).
t=9: W to (6,6). Deliver P1.
t=10: W to (5,6).
t=11: W to (4,6).
t=12: W to (3,6).
t=13: W to (2,6).
End of Plan 3: Position (2,6) at t=13.
Then Plan 4: S, S, E, E, E. (5 moves).
t=13: S to (2,7).
t=14: S to (2,8).
t=15: E to (3,8).
t=16: E to (4,8).
t=17: E to (5,8).
End of Plan 4: Position (5,8) at t=18.
Then Plan 5: E, S, S, E, E. (5 moves).
t=18: E to (6,8).
t=19: S to (6,7).
t=20: S to (6,6). (Wait, I delivered P1 at t=9, so I don't need to go back to (6,6) unless I'm confused).
Ah, I need to go to (9,5).
From (6,6) at t=9.
Plan 4 (revised): W, W, W, W, W.
t=9: W to (5,6).
t=10: W to (4,6).
t=11: W to (3,6).
t=12: W to (2,6).
t=13: W to (1,6).
End of Plan 4: Position (1,6) at t=14.

Path: (6,6) -> (7,6) -> (8,6) -> (8,7) -> (8,8) -> (9,8) -> (9,7) -> (9,6) -> (9,5).

t=9: E to (7,6).
t=10: E to (8,6). (Guard P at 8,6 at t=10? 10 mod 8 = 2. Yes, P is at (8,6). BUMP/CAUGHT).

I must wait or take a different route.

t=9: S to (6,7).

t=11: E to (7,8). (Guard P at 8,7). Safe.

t=16: N to (9,5). (Guard P at 8,6). Safe.
Pick up P2 at t=16.
Then deliver to (4,7).
Path: (9,5) -> W...W to (4,5) -> S, S.
Steps: W, W, W, W, W, S, S. (7 steps).
Start at (9,5) at t=16.
t=16: W to (8,5). (Guard P at 8,5 at t=16? 16 mod 8 = 0. P is at (8,4). Safe).
t=17: W to (7,5). (Guard P at 8,5). Safe.
t=18: W to (6,5). (Guard P at 8,6). Safe.
t=19: W to (5,5). (Guard P at 8,5). Safe.
t=20: W to (4,5). (Guard P at 8,6). Safe.
t=21: S to (4,6). (Guard P at 8,5). Safe.
t=22: S to (4,7). (Guard P at 8,7). Safe.
Deliver P2 at t=22.
Total time 22 ticks.
Now I need to structure this into 5-move plans.
Plan 1: E E E E E.

t=0: (3,8).
t=1: (4,8).

t=6: S to (8,7).

Your last response could not be processed: missing or malformed POSITION line.

action.content:

First, I need to reach (8,8). The direct path along row 8 is clear of walls.
Guard Q cycles every 4 ticks: t=0:(1,8), t=1:(2,8), t=2:(3,8), t=3:(2,8).
If I move East immediately:
t=0: Move E to (3,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
t=1: Move E to (4,8). Guard Q moves to (3,8). Safe.
t=2: Move E to (5,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
t=3: Move E to (6,8). Guard Q moves to (3,8). Safe.
t=4: Move E to (7,8). Guard Q moves to (2,8). Safe.
At the end of this plan (t=5), I am at (7,8).
Next, I need to go to (8,8) to pick up Parcel 1.
t=5: Move E to (8,8). Pick up Parcel 1.
t=6: Move S to (8,7).
t=7: Move S to (8,6).
At the end of this plan (t=8), I am at (8,6).
Now I need to deliver Parcel 1 to (6,6).
From (8,6), I can go West along row 6.
t=8: Move W to (7,6).
t=9: Move W to (6,6). Deliver Parcel 1.
t=10: Move W to (5,6).
t=11: Move W to (4,6).
t=12: Move W to (3,6).
At the end of this plan (t=13), I am at (3,6).
Now I need to go to (9,5) to pick up Parcel 2.
From (3,6), I can go West to (1,6), then South to (1,7), then East along row 7? No, (5,7) is a wall.
Let's re-evaluate the path from (3,6) to (9,5).
Option A: Go South to (3,7), then East to (4,7) (Guard Q is at (2,8) at t=13, moves to (3,8) at t=14, (2,8) at t=15, (3,8) at t=16).
Wait, Guard Q at t=13 (13 mod 4 = 1) is at (2,8).
t=14: Q at (3,8).
t=15: Q at (2,8).
t=16: Q at (3,8).
t=17: Q at (2,8).
If I go S to (3,7) at t=13, then E to (4,7) at t=14.
(4,7) is 'y', which is the delivery spot for Parcel 2. But I haven't picked up Parcel 2 yet. I need to go to (9,5) first.
So going to (4,7) early is a detour unless I can backtrack.
Let's try going South from (3,6) to (3,7), then East to (4,7), (5,7) is blocked.
So I must go around the wall at (5,7).
The wall is at (5,7).
Path from (3,6) to (9,5):
(3,6) -> S -> (3,7) -> E -> (4,7) -> S -> (4,6) -> E -> (5,6) -> E -> (6,6) -> E -> (7,6) -> E -> (8,6) -> E -> (9,6) -> S -> (9,5).
This seems long.
Alternative: (3,6) -> W -> (2,6) -> W -> (1,6) -> S -> (1,7) -> E -> (2,7) -> E -> (3,7) -> S -> (3,6) ... loop.
Let's look at the map again.
Row 7: . S . y # . # . .
Cols: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9
(5,7) is #. (7,7) is #.
So row 7 is blocked at 5 and 7.
Row 6: all dots.
Row 8: all dots.
Row 9: . # # . . . # . .
(2,9)#, (3,9)#, (7,9)#.
So to get to (9,5), I can come from (9,6) or (8,5).
(8,5) is in row 5, which is all dots.
So path: (3,6) -> W -> (2,6) -> W -> (1,6) -> S -> (1,7) -> E -> (2,7) -> E -> (3,7) -> S -> (3,6) ... no.
Let's try: (3,6) -> S -> (3,7) -> E -> (4,7) -> S -> (4,6) -> E -> (5,6) -> E -> (6,6) -> E -> (7,6) -> E -> (8,6) -> E -> (9,6) -> S -> (9,5).
This path avoids walls.
Let's check Guard P.
P cycle: 0:(8,4), 1:(8,5), 2:(8,6), 3:(8,7), 4:(9,7), 5:(8,7), 6:(8,6), 7:(8,5).
At t=13, I am at (3,6).
t=13: S to (3,7). P at (8,5). Safe.
t=14: E to (4,7). P at (8,6). Safe.
t=15: S to (4,6). P at (8,5). Safe.
